data.title: Millicom International Cellular S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Millicom International Cellular S.A. press release (TIGO): FY Net profit for the year of $1.36B.
  • Revenue of $5.82B (+0.3% Y/Y).

data.title: Indian refiners secure 60M barrels of Russian oil; Reliance adds 5M barrels of Iranian crude after U.S. waiver
data.description:
Indian refiners have reportedly bought about 60 million barrels of Russian oil for delivery next month, easing supply concerns as the Middle East war chokes flows. The cargoes were booked at premiums of $5 to $15 a barrel to Brent, according to a report by Bloomberg News. 

The volume is similar to the amount of purchases for this month but more than double that for February, the report said, citing data intelligence firm Kpler.

India's Reliance Industries, operator of the world's biggest refining complex, has meanwhile purchased 5 million ​barrels of Iranian crude, days after the U.S. temporarily removed sanctions on ‌the oil, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters. The Indian refiner bought the oil from the National Iranian Oil Co., two of the sources ​said.

One of them said the crude was priced at a ​premium of about $7 a barrel to ICE Brent futures. It ⁠was not immediately clear when the oil would be delivered.

The United States earlier this month issued a 30-day waiver to India for purchasing crude from Moscow as the Iran war upends global supplies. The waiver on purchasing Russian oil is expected to help ease global supply worries, with India being the world’s fourth-biggest refiner and fifth-largest exporter of petroleum products.

data.title: Blackstone, David Blitzer bet big on Indian Premier League with $1.8B RCB buy
data.description:
A consortium backed by serial American sports investor David Blitzer and Blackstone (BX) has agreed to acquire Indian Premier League ((IPL)) franchise Royal Challengers Bengaluru, or RCB, in a deal valued at roughly $1.8B.

The investor group includes India’s Aditya Birla Group, Times of India Group, Blitzer’s Bolt Ventures, and Blackstone’s perpetual private equity strategy, BXPE (BXPE). 

The RCB franchise is currently owned by United Spirits Limited, the Indian arm of UK-based drinks giant Diageo (DEO), which has opted to divest the asset, calling it "non-core" to its primary alcohol business. To note, RCB, one of the IPL’s founding teams, has seen a significant uplift in brand value following its maiden title win in 2025.

The deal, which also includes the team’s women’s side competing in the Women’s Premier League, is subject to regulatory approvals from the Board of Control for Cricket in India and the Competition Commission of India.

data.title: TotalEnergies says about 15% of output offline but surging oil prices more than offset losses
data.description:
Roughly 15% of TotalEnergies’ (TTE) production is offline, as the war with Iran nears the one-month mark, but surging oil prices have more than made up for the lost barrels, chairman and CEO Patrick Pouyanné told CNBC in an interview.

With Brent crude (CO1:COM) trading above $100 a barrel, much of the attention has focused on oil prices, but Pouyanné said the crisis is having a much larger impact on product prices.

“The Brent market is ok, but the products market, which is the one which impacts customers … is much higher than Brent,” he told CNBC at S&P Global’s CERAWeek energy conference in Houston.

He added, the world has “never experienced” refining margins from products including Asian jet fuel at current levels. In addition to petroleum products, about 30% of global fertilizer moves through the Strait of Hormuz, jeopardizing the spring planting season.

TotalEnergies (TTE) is a major player in the global LNG market, including the largest exporter of U.S. LNG. The CEO said the company can still fulfill customer orders in Europe and Asia thanks to its diversified global portfolio.

Pouyanné expects prices could move substantially higher if the war drags on through the summer, since Asian demand rises over the summer just as Europe looks to refill storage. European natural gas traded around $18 per million British thermal units Tuesday, but Pouyanné said prices could hit $40/MMBtu over the summer if the conflict continues.

Last week, QatarEnergy said its Ras Laffan plant suffered “extensive damage” following Iranian drone attacks, effectively taking 20% of global LNG supply offline. The shutdown has sent natural gas prices in Europe and Asia surging.

Shares linked to U.S. natural gas rose Tuesday as the Iran war disrupts Middle East supplies. Notable movers included Venture Global (VG), Diversified Energy (DEC), Cheniere Energy (LNG), NextDecade (NEXT), Expand Energy (EXE), Antero Resources (AR), Coterra Energy (CTRA), and EQT (EQT), with gains ranging from about 1.5% to 8%.

ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Asian markets surge as U.S.-Iran peace plan tumbles oil prices
data.description:
Asian equity markets advanced on Wednesday amid reports of a possible US-proposed ceasefire with Iran, which helped ease concerns over oil prices.

Market sentiment remains cautiously optimistic yet mixed following reports that the US was pursuing a one-month ceasefire to enable talks. According to The New York Times, Washington sent Iran a 15-point proposal to resolve the conflict. These efforts were more significant than worries about increased tensions in the Middle East following President Trump's order for 2,000 troops to be deployed.

WTI crude futures dropped about 4% to below $89 per barrel on Wednesday, reversing the previous session’s gains.

Gold prices climbed more than 2% toward $4,600 on Wednesday, extending gains from the previous session.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 2.80% to above 53,600, while the broader Topix Index climbed 2.3% to 3,642 on Wednesday. The Japanese yen steadied around 158.7 per dollar on Wednesday after a volatile start to the week.

The KOSPI (KOSPI) rose over 3% on Wednesday due to better outlooks for resolving the Middle East conflict. SK Hynix surged more than 5% after considering a US listing to meet rising AI memory demand. 

China (SHCOMP) rose 1.08% to above 3,900, while the Shenzhen Component jumped 1.9% to 13,800, extending gains from the previous session, and the offshore yuan 

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.37% to around 25,284 in Wednesday trading, marking a second straight session of gains.

India (SENSEX) rose 2.19% to 75,042 on Wednesday, extending gains from the previous session. The Indian rupee hovered just below 94 per dollar, steadying near record low levels. Market participants continue to watch crude price swings, foreign flows, and geopolitical developments.

On the data front, India’s HSBC composite PMI flash fell to 56.5 in March from 58.9 in February and below market expectations of 58.7, marking the weakest expansion since October 2022 amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. 

Australia (AS51) rose 1.85% to 8,486 in Wednesday morning trade, extending gains for a second session. The Australian dollar edged down to around $0.70 on Wednesday, approaching a two-week low.

Australia's annual inflation decreased to 3.7% in February 2026, slightly below the expected 3.8%, but still above the central bank's target of 2–3%.

In the U.S. on Tuesday, all three major indexes ended lower as a resurgent energy rally and conflicting Middle East headlines stalled previous momentum.

Investors now turn to US export and import price data due Wednesday, along with earnings from Chewy, Paychex, and others.

U.S. stock futures advanced on Wednesday following reports that Washington was pursuing talks with Iran to end the conflict: Dow +0.77%; S&P 500 +0.67%; Nasdaq +0.69%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: MaxCyte outlines $30M–$32M 2026 revenue target as ExPERT DTx launch and SPL milestones drive outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: MaxCyte (MXCT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Maher Masoud, President, CEO & Executive Director, highlighted that 2025 was a challenging year but also one of "meaningful progress for MaxCyte." The company acquired SeQure DX, integrated the business, and launched the ExPERT DTx platform. Masoud stated, "We made meaningful changes to rightsize spending and strategically improve our operations. And most recently, we launched a new product into ExPERT DTx that will allow us to work with developers earlier in research and filament discovery."
  • Masoud reported $33 million of total revenue for the full year, including $29.6 million of core revenue and $3.4 million of strategic Platform License Program revenue. He noted the installed instrument base grew to 857 units from 760 at the end of 2024.
  • The ExPERT DTx launch targets earlier-stage research and drug discovery, offering a modular 96-well electroporation platform designed to streamline workflows and facilitate progression from discovery to GMP-compliant manufacturing.
  • Masoud outlined 2026 revenue guidance: "we expect total revenue to be in the range of $30 million to $32 million, consisting of $25 million to $27 million of core revenue and $5 million of SPL program-related revenue." He acknowledged a $4 million core revenue headwind from SPL customer program rationalization and a recent license termination, stating, "we expect Q1 to be our lightest quarter for core revenue with the back half weighted year."
  • The CEO emphasized an ongoing commitment to innovation and a belief that "the headwinds facing our business are a result of the conservation of capital by biotechs... which we expect to stabilize in the second half of 2026."
  • On management changes, Masoud announced, "Parmeet Ahuja will be joining MaxCyte as Chief Financial Officer, succeeding Doug Swirsky effective March 30."
  • CFO Douglas Swirsky reported, "Total revenue for the full year was $33 million compared to $38.6 million in 2024, representing a 15% decline." He highlighted a decrease in both core and SPL program-related revenue, and detailed, "Gross margin was 78% in the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to 74% in the fourth quarter of 2024." Swirsky also noted, "We ended 2025 with combined total cash and cash equivalents and investments of $155.6 million and no debt."

Outlook

  • Guidance for 2026 is $30 million to $32 million in total revenue, with $25 million to $27 million core and $5 million SPL program-related revenue. Masoud explained, "Included in our guidance is the impact of the recent notice received from an SPL customer terminating their license for reasons unrelated to our platform's performance, along with approximately $4 million in core revenue headwind from Select SPL customers."
  • Masoud stated, "We continue to believe that the headwinds facing our business are a result of the conservation of capital by biotechs... which we expect to stabilize in the second half of 2026 and growth from that new base."
  • He expects "revenue growth from non-SPL customers, which is inclusive of growth from security apps," and noted a $2 million royalty revenue expectation from a commercial stage SPL customer in 2026.

Financial Results

  • Swirsky detailed, "Total revenue in the fourth quarter of 2025 was $7.3 million compared to $8.7 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, representing a 16% decline."
  • Core revenue for Q4 2025 was $6.8 million versus $8.6 million in Q4 2024, including $1.8 million in instrument revenue, $2 million in license revenue, and $2.3 million in PA revenue for the recent quarter.
  • For the full year, core revenue was $29.6 million (down from $32.5 million). SeQure DX contributed $1.1 million in total 2025 revenue.
  • Operating expenses for Q4 2025 were $16.9 million, compared to $19.3 million in Q4 2024, with cost savings attributed to restructuring and efficiency initiatives. Swirsky further announced, "we anticipate at least $136 million in cash equivalents and investments at the end of 2026."

Q&A

  • Daniel Arias, Stifel: "Why is the core business expected to decline more this year than it did last year? Are you losing share somewhere?" Masoud responded, "Really, the headwinds we're facing is $4 million. And it comes down to... it's not a distribution of our business in any way. It's not a change in the fundamentals of our business anyway."
  • Arias followed up: "Does the outlook for core revenues assume that the industry demand dynamic improves over the course of the year?" Masoud replied, "that would be upside what we have right now... If there's further industry demand that's upside from where we're guiding."
  • Matthew Hewitt, Craig-Hallum: "What does that [ExPERT DTx] pipeline look like? And how quickly do you think you could see that start to trickle into the revenues?" Masoud said, "We see it trickling into revenues in the second half... we're seeing right now some DTx is being sold right now in the first quarter before it's even over."
  • Hewitt: "How do you account for or how do you factor that into your guidance? What kind of a haircut do you take on those -- the potential for those milestones?" Masoud replied, "we already received one milestone, a 7-trigger milestone in Q1 this year... The very least will be 2 of those 4, but potential for 4 this year."
  • Unknown Analyst, William Blair: "SPL cadence... how you're expecting for this year and perpetuity?" Masoud stated, "We feel good about signing at least that 3 this year as well in that 3 to 5 range."
  • Vidyun Bais, BTIG: "What's baked in as far as Secure contribution?... speak to your level of visibility and confidence in that, just given it's from a partner therapy?" Masoud explained, "we don't break it up in terms of -- in the guide itself other than the fact we see material growth year-over-year for Secure in 2026 versus 2025... we expect approximately $2 million of revenue from commercial royalty."
  • Steven Etoch, Stephens: "What you're hearing from customers in terms of macro environment... demand throughout FY '26?" Masoud addressed, "this is not so much any more of a demand issue or a customer funding issue. This is about the headwinds we saw in -- that affect us in Q1, this is more of a second half weighted guide."
  • Chad Wiatrowski, TD Cowen: "Are those [DTx] mostly existing customers enjoying the convenience of that? Or is this something that enables more newer customers?" Masoud: "This is a mix of both. This is not just for current customers, it's also for new customers."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was skeptical, with questions focused on the rationale for continued revenue headwinds and the disconnect between industry trends and MaxCyte's guidance. Arias remarked, "I really don't understand the trajectory of the business right now." Other analysts pressed for clarity on new product ramp and SPL cadence.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, consistently emphasizing the transitory nature of headwinds and the robustness of the SPL pipeline. Masoud repeatedly stated confidence in the outlook: "I feel very good where we are."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone shifted toward more direct skepticism about projected declines, while management's tone remained steady and focused on long-term opportunity.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is lower than the prior year, with management now projecting $30 million to $32 million in total revenue, reflecting ongoing customer program rationalization and a license termination. The prior quarter maintained a guidance range of flat to 10% core revenue decline for 2025 and SPL program-related revenue of approximately $5 million.
  • Strategic focus remains on SPL program growth and the ExPERT DTx launch, with added emphasis this quarter on realizing revenue from later-stage SPL programs and commercial royalties.
  • The previous quarter included significant cost reductions and restructuring, which have now materialized in lower operating expenses and anticipated reduced cash burn in 2026.
  • Management's confidence in the SPL pipeline and new product ramp is consistent, though analysts' concerns about disconnects with broader biopharma trends have intensified.

Risks and Concerns

  • Specific challenges cited include a $4 million headwind from SPL customer program consolidation and rationalization, a recent license termination, and the impact of biotechs' capital conservation.
  • Management's mitigation strategies involve rightsizing spending, restructuring for efficiency, and focusing on innovation and product launches like ExPERT DTx to drive future growth.
  • Analysts raised concerns about MaxCyte's ability to return to growth in the context of improving industry dynamics and questioned the sufficiency of new product ramps to offset headwinds.

Final Takeaway

MaxCyte enters 2026 with a leaner cost structure, new product offerings, and a focus on SPL program expansion. While revenue guidance reflects ongoing headwinds from customer program consolidation, management underscores stabilization in the second half of the year and growth driven by new SPLs, milestone payments, and the traction of ExPERT DTx. The company maintains a strong cash position and expects continued progress as pivotal-stage programs advance and commercial royalties ramp throughout the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Apogee Therapeutics prices upsized $350M offering at $70 a share
Portofino Resources appoints Rodney Campbell as interim CEO
OpenAI secures more funding, lifting record round to $120B, CFO says
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) is raising an additional $10 billion from investors as part of its historic funding round, CFO Sarah Friar told CNBC’s Jim Cramer on Tuesday.

The fresh capital brings OpenAI’s record fundraise to “north of $120 billion,” Friar said in an interview on “Mad Money.” That well exceeds the ChatGPT creator’s initial target of $100 billion. OpenAI announced the first tranche of investment in late February, and it’s been considered possibly its last private fundraise before a potential blockbuster initial public offering.

Andreessen Horowitz, D.E. Shaw Ventures, MGX, TPG and T. Rowe Price are participants in the new $10 billion commitment, according to Friar.

Notably, Microsoft (MSFT), a longtime investor in OpenAI and one of its major computing providers, is also joining this part of the funding round. While the relationship between Microsoft and OpenAI has evolved, Friar called Microsoft “an incredible partner” and complimented its CEO, Satya Nadella, for being “there early.”

“What I’m really pleased about is we raised money all around the ecosystem,” Friar told Cramer, pointing to involve from venture capital firms, private equity players, mutual funds and sovereign entities. “It didn’t matter where you went, people really believed in this AI revolution and they wanted to put their money to work behind it,” she added.

The comments come as a document that resembles an IPO prospectus, OpenAI (OPENAI) has reportedly said its close ties with Microsoft (MSFT) could be a potential risk to its business, telling investors that the software company is responsible for “a substantial portion of our financing and compute.”

Last month, OpenAI announced $110 billion in funding from strategic partners including Amazon (AMZN), Nvidia (NVDA), and SoftBank (SFTBY).

data.title: Google DeepMind teams up with Agile Robots for AI robotics push
data.description:
Google (GOOG) DeepMind has landed a partnership with Agile Robots, marking its latest move to deepen its footprint in AI-driven robotics.

The Munich-based firm has over 20,000 systems deployed globally and aims to embed DeepMind's multimodal AI models—capable of processing vision, language, and tactile data—into its industrial robots. 

"By bringing together Agile Robots’ hardware and other AI robotic solutions developed in Germany, with Google DeepMind’s Gemini Robotics foundation models, the two teams will improve performance via robot deployment, data collection, model training and iteration." Agile Robots said in a statement.

The companies will jointly test, refine, and deploy these AI-powered robots across sectors such as electronics manufacturing, automotive, logistics, and data centers.

Founded in 2018, Agile Robots has secured over $270M in venture funding from investors including SoftBank Vision Fund, Xiaomi, and Midas Group, among others. 

The move is part of Google’s (GOOG) broader push into “physical AI,” where advanced models are applied to real-world machines. Earlier this year, Google (GOOG) collaborated with robotics firms like Boston Dynamics to help develop its upcoming humanoid robot Atlas. Boston Dynamics was previously owned by Google (GOOG) from 2013 to 2017.
